item.title: Nancy Guthrie's disappearance remains mystery four months after she vanished
item.description: It has been four months since Nancy Guthrie, the mother of America's Today show co-host Savannah Guthrie, was reported missing. This is what we know about the alleged abduction.

item.title: Adelaide Hills man Bo De-Simone pleads guilty to murder of mother Linda Simon
item.description: A man who previously denied murdering his mother at their home in 2022, and even gave evidence in his own defence, has a last-minute change of heart and pleads guilty to murder.

item.title: Chadstone cold case accused Garry Sullivan faces court three decades after armed robbery
item.description: A 78-year-old man accused of shooting three people in an Armaguard robbery at a busy cinema in 1994 faces court.

item.title: Why Israel's Beaufort Castle seizure is historically and strategically significant
item.description: Israel has captured a castle in southern Lebanon in a mountainous military siege that marked Israeli forces' deepest incursion into the country in 26 years.

item.title: Easy and affordable curries to bring some winter warmth
item.description: Curries are perfect for cooler months, and many come with a serving of immune-boosting properties.

item.title: Reconciliation Week puts spotlight on Welcome to Country tradition after divisive Anzac Day services
item.description: Indigenous elders explain why they believe Welcome to Country ceremonies have faced opposition, and why abandoning the practice would create a cultural "abyss".

item.title: $6 million of cocaine found near burnt-out truck at boat ramp in Whitsundays
item.description: Packages of cocaine estimated to be worth $6 million have been found floating in the sea and near the remains of a burnt-out truck at an isolated north Queensland boat ramp.

item.title: Australia wins women's and men's rugby sevens final at World Championship leg in Spain
item.description: Australia puts itself in pole position to win the women's rugby sevens World Championship, while the men's team also enjoys the spoils of victory in Spain.

item.title: Police to search 1,000 mine shafts, drain dam in renewed search for Trisha Graf
item.description: A renewed search for 41-year-old Trisha Graf will include searching about 1,000 mine shafts and draining Blue Dam.

item.title: Teenagers on NSW Central Coast create student-led Northwave music festival
item.description: High school students have put on a music festival as part of their studies to get them into the industry.

item.title: International Ebola cases climbing 'fast' but Australia won't impose travel restrictions
item.description: The federal government will not impose border restrictions on travellers from Ebola-stricken countries despite new suspected cases in Italy and Brazil, as the worsening outbreak prompts other countries to take action.

item.title: NT WorkSafe charges health department over death of mental health patient at RDH
item.description: WorkSafe charges Northern Territory Health with failing to comply with health and safety duties over the death of a mental health in-patient at Royal Darwin Hospital in 2022.

item.title: Maroons lose Pat Carrigan and Gehamat Shibasaki ahead of State of Origin II against Blues
item.description: Queensland's task of levelling the State of Origin series has become more difficult after confirmation two of its stars have been ruled out of Game II at the MCG.

item.title: Euphoria season 3 finale recap: Guns, drugs, the bible and Rue's fate
item.description: Here's your recap of season 3, episode 8 of Euphoria. There are spoilers. You have been warned.

item.title: Buyers of Wieambilla property tell owner they will respect the lives lost
item.description: The son of Wieambilla police killers says the new owners of the property are open to maintaining a memorial at the site to the slain officers.

item.title: Mark Sheridan Waden faces trial over alleged murder of US beauty queen Priscilla Brooten
item.description: The murder trial of Mark Sheridan Waden has heard allegations he killed his then-partner Priscilla Brooten during an argument almost eight years ago, after the American former beauty queen learned he was having an affair with a colleague.

item.title: US and Iran exchange strikes amid negotiations to end war
item.description: The United States says it struck Iranian military and Iran says it responded by targeting a US base.

item.title: Sunshine Coast police investigate nine-week-old baby's head injuries
item.description: Queensland Police are investigating after a nine-week-old baby was taken to hospital earlier this year with "serious" head injuries.

item.title: Woman rescued after getting stuck in quicksand at Glenelg North shares warning
item.description: A relaxing sunset walk at the beach with a friend turned to panic for a young woman after her leg got stuck in quicksand, prompting a rescue from police and fire crews.

item.title: 13yo Queensland boy charged with violent extremist offences
item.description: Queensland Police say a 13-year-old boy posed an "imminent threat" to a Maryborough school.

item.title: AI chatbots accused of helping to encourage violent attacks and at the centre of US lawsuits
item.description: An investigation into how AI chatbots respond to violent requests produces "deeply disturbing" results, as OpenAI denies ChatGPT encourages crime.

item.title: Influencer recorded chat with Bankstown nurses for 'protection', court told
item.description: An Israeli content creator involved in an online video chat where two Sydney nurses allegedly threatened Israeli patients tells a court he recorded his chats for his own protection and published their conversation because he was "really worried about their statements".

item.title: Maua Lagaaia pleads not guilty in Supreme Court trial after 2023 death of Lincoln Hoad
item.description: It was a "boys' trip to the races" that went wrong, a Supreme Court jury in Toowoomba has heard.

item.title: Identity of man at centre of alleged extramarital relationship extortion remains suppressed
item.description: The name of a high-profile Queenslander entwined in an extortion charge must temporarily remain under wraps while police prepare an application to prevent it from becoming public knowledge.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: 'Awestruck' Grenvold bolts into the Diamonds squad
item.description: Despite signing her first full-time Super Netball contract this year there was no guarantee Jessie Grenvold would get a lot of court time. But through injury, the Mavericks defender took on a starting spot and is now an Australian Diamond.

item.title: The disturbing role of ChatGPT in mass shootings
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Oxfam research finds record number of Australian billionaires hold total wealth of $686b
item.description: Australia now has 178 billionaires who collectively hold more than $686 billion in wealth, sparking debate about the advantages and disadvantages of the growing number of ultra-wealthy.

item.title: Iranian state media reports peace talks with US suspended but Trump insists they continue at 'rapid pace'
item.description: Iranian state media is reporting that the country's government has halted negotiations with the United States over an end to the war in the Middle East, in protest over Israel's conduct in Lebanon.

item.title: Scripps Spelling Bee quiz: Do you know what these words mean?
item.description: We don't think you'll have even heard of most of these words, let alone know how to spell them.

item.title: Why make-up sex may not be fixing anything
item.description: Experts say if a fight keeps ending up in the bedroom, it could be a sign you and your partner aren't resolving anything.

item.title: Carly Sheppard unites with Alisdair Macindoe for The Shepherds at the Australian Dance Biennale
item.description: Carly Sheppard is perhaps best known for experimental performance, but a coincidental meeting with a fellow dancer convinced her to go back to her roots.

item.title: When police charged footballer Tom Starling with assault, one detective broke ranks
item.description: As the footballer, shirt torn, face bloodied and with a concussion, paced his police cell, a veteran detective's phone started to ring.

item.title: How batteries muscled out gas and pushed power prices down
item.description: Fossil fuels, which have powered the global economy for centuries, are under threat. And Australia has become ground zero in this global battle.

item.title: Workmate of missing Queensland man Jack McLennan says he's 'given police absolutely everything'
item.description: The last known person to see missing Queensland man Jack McLennan alive has broken his public silence amid intense community speculation over his role in Mr McLennan's disappearance.

item.title: The impact of Australia only buying second-hand US submarines under AUKUS
item.description: Australia will only buy second-hand Virginia nuclear-powered submarines from the US during the AUKUS stopgap phase, with some analysts criticising the move and others believing it is a positive change of plan.

item.title: Even Hanson knows polling isn't everything but major parties have their work cut out
item.description: The One Nation leader, whose popularity has soared in the past 12 months, would be the first to say she doesn't put too much stock in the numbers.

item.title: Antibiotic florfenicol detected at 'low level' 14km from fish farm site where it was used
item.description: Tasmania's Natural Resources and Environment Department has confirmed the antibiotic florfenicol was detected at a 'low level' in wild seafood 14 kilometres from where the drug was administered.

item.title: Birds return to Little Dog Island in Bass Strait after feral cats eradicated
item.description: Penguins have been sighted on a remote Bass Strait island for the first time in decades after the successful eradication of feral cats and possums.

item.title: Voters consider alternatives to major political parties as One Nation popularity surges
item.description: Pauline Hanson's right-wing populist party is leading opinion polls for the first time, and regional Queenslanders say they can see why.

item.title: Emirates proposes increase in helicopter flights at luxury Wolgan Valley resort
item.description: A luxury resort that has been shut for three years hopes to reopen by chauffeuring guests down a steep 4WD track and flying them in on more than 200 helicopter flights a week.

item.title: Former wife of tobacco baron alleges horrific life entangled in illegal trade
item.description: After an arranged marriage to tobacco baron Sam Albion, Kawther Salih says the relationship spiralled into an abusive situation that saw her subjugated into working in the underground tobacco industry.

item.title: 'Daniel' is a fake AI real estate agent who is trying to sell units to strangers
item.description: "Daniel" is an AI real estate agent who calls strangers and secretly records conversations without their consent.

item.title: Laos cave rescue team member's journey from soldier to comedian to diver
item.description: Josh Richards has made news for his diving and his interplanetary ambitions. Now he's using his skills to try to rescue others.

item.title: Council uses AI to inform 10-year plan for 'more depth' at 'fifth of the price'
item.description: A Tasmanian council says using AI for a strategic plan has saved them time and money, and will be used much more in the future, but a resident says the community consultation is 'completely lost with AI'.

item.title: Serena Williams announces return to professional tennis with Queen's Club doubles wildcard
item.description: The greatest women's champion of the Open Era is returning to tennis after almost four years away, accepting a wildcard into the doubles at the Queen's Club tournament next week.

item.title: Katherine residents say potential damages from PFAS firefighting foam case should pay for further 'recompense'
item.description: Katherine residents have welcomed a $2 billion lawsuit over PFAS contamination in the town, saying "it's about time we started holding large corporations to account".

item.title: Victorian watchdog set up to monitor farmland restoration amid mineral sands boom
item.description: Mineral sands projects in the Wimmera Mallee will be assessed by a new governance group, to ensure land restoration plans are appropriate.

item.title: Changes to Victorian law to provide 'tougher scrutiny' after Monash IVF bungles
item.description: The government is introducing amendments to laws governing IVF clinics that will allow the state's health minister to personally intervene to cancel a provider's registration.

item.title: Farmers flock to buy CCTV cameras to stop fuel theft
item.description: Fuel theft is prompting farmers to install CCTV cameras at unprecedented rates, leading one security company to overhaul its entire business model.

item.title: Real pay for millions of workers still catching up to 2021, ASX slips — as it happened
item.description: Millions of workers will get a 4.75 per cent pay rise after Fair Work raised modern award wages above the rate of inflation. Catch up on the day's financial news and insights from our specialist business reporters on our blog.

item.title: Aryna Sabalenka rolls past Naomi Osaka at French Open as dream runs continue
item.description: A series of upsets across Roland Garros leaves world number one Aryna Sabalenka in the box seat to win the claycourt grand slam after beating Naomi Osaka.

item.title: Race to develop Ebola vaccine heats up amid deadly outbreak in DRC
item.description: Nearly 250 people are suspected to have died, and more than 1,100 are infected with the rare strain of Ebola in the DRC and Uganda, though the true spread of the virus is feared to be much wider.

item.title: Non-disclosure agreements to be waived for landmark military sexual violence inquiry
item.description: Gag orders imposed on former and current Defence personnel who experienced sexual violence will not be enforced, allowing them to participate in a landmark military sexual violence inquiry.

item.title: Police search for man who fired shots into a house in Melbourne's north last year
item.description: A man was filmed firing three shots at a house in Melbourne's north in what police believe was a targeted incident last year.

item.title: Domestic violence survivors tell stories through art in Toowoomba
item.description: Dozens of women share their experience of domestic violence through art.

item.title: Workers strike at Inpex Ichthys operation after negotiations on pay deal stall
item.description: Australian workers at the Japanese gas company Inpex walked off the job this morning, and plan to strike again this evening.

item.title: Meg Lanning gets AI slop treatment in Manchester Super Giants promotional video
item.description: The controversial takeover of The Hundred franchise, Manchester Super Giants, continues with a promotional video criticised for seemingly being entirely AI-generated.

item.title: Allegedly stolen vehicle crashes north of Katherine, killing teenage boy
item.description: A teenager has died after the allegedly stolen vehicle he was in rolled on a major Northern Territory highway early today.

item.title: Fair Work Commission annual ruling lifts minimum wage by 6 per cent and award rate by 4.75 per cent
item.description: Australia's minimum wage will increase by 5.97 per cent, and minimum award workers will get a 4.75 per cent pay boost, in the Fair Work Commission's (FWC) annual wage review.

item.title: Steady is a joy, but raising a dog wasn't what I'd imagined
item.description: I completely neglected to give much thought to whether my personality would slot into a dog's lifestyle. Spoiler — it did not.

item.title: ABARES report predicts broadacre farm profits will drop by 70 per cent
item.description: The ABARES report for the June quarter predicts national crop production will fall by more than 20 per cent, with profits down 70 per cent for broadacre farms.

item.title: French Open boss Amelie Mauresmo stands firm on line judges after Casper Ruud controversy
item.description: Despite the other grand slams relying on technology for their line calls, French Open director Amelie Mauresmo is still happy with her human line judges despite a controversial miss costing Casper Ruud.

item.title: Former ACM executive Antony Catalano's bail conditions varied to allow interstate travel
item.description: Former ACM executive Antony Catalano will be allowed to visit his wife and kids after successfully applying to have his bail conditions changed.

item.title: New North Canberra Hospital details unveiled as $1.34 billion committed to redevelopment
item.description: The ACT government unveils details of the new North Canberra Hospital as part of its latest budget, with the total cost of the project now expected to be $1.5 billion.

item.title: Major Russian attack on Ukraine leaves at least 22 dead and dozens injured
item.description: Moscow targeted Kyiv and other cities, killing multiple people, damaging buildings and leaving wounded trapped under rubble, local authorities say.

item.title: Woman credits 'divine intervention' after tree hits car during WA's strongest storm in 49 years
item.description: A mother shares her incredible tale of survival after a tree falls on her car in a freak accident on a South West highway during WA's strongest storm in 49 years.

item.title: NSW releases Cultural Fire Strategy to support Aboriginal-led burning
item.description: The NSW Cultural Fire Strategy has set a clear timeline to better resource and support Indigenous-led cultural burning.

item.title: Charge dropped against veteran broadcaster Alan Jones ahead of indecent assault trial
item.description: Prosecutors drop one of the criminal charges against veteran broadcaster Alan Jones less than two months before the start of his trial, reducing the number of complainants in his indecent assault case.

item.title: Royal Hobart Hospital ban on colourful, 'fun' scrubs for nurses set to be overturned
item.description: Nurses at the Royal Hobart Hospital have been told they should stop wearing fun scrubs or "free dress" and reminded they need to maintain "some professionalism" regarding their uniform.

item.title: Health minister asked if nurses have been directed to stop wearing fun scrubs
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Quicksand causes terrifying ‘Indiana Jones moment’ for second Adelaide beachgoer
item.description: Days after a young Adelaide beachgoer had to be rescued from quicksand, a second person has spoken out about a similar experience along the same stretch of coast, saying she was momentarily overcome by "complete fear".

item.title: Farmers say new rules for feral deer hunting in Tasmania are a 'missed opportunity'
item.description: The Tasmanian government is making changes to the rules for deer hunting in the state, but farmers say it won't be enough to bring numbers down, with the sector's peak body calling for the species to be declared a pest.

item.title: Ukrainian building in ruins after Russian attack on Dnipro kills four
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Visa applicants out of pocket as Gold Migration Lawyers goes into liquidation
item.description: Gold Migration Lawyers went into liquidation yesterday, leaving customers out of pocket thousands of dollars and their visa applications unresolved.

item.title: Weather warning for abnormally high tides along entire SA coastline
item.description: South Australia's State Emergency Service warns people not to risk their safety as abnormally high tides are set to lash the state's coastline.

item.title: Kieran Foran named Sea Eagles's full-time NRL coach, Chiefs and Dragons sign Connor Watson
item.description: Manly shows faith in Kieran Foran by appointing the ex-NRL star as its head coach through to the end of the 2029 season.

item.title: Trump's intervention in Netanyahu's plans to bomb Beirut reminds world who's calling shots in Iran
item.description: Donald Trump's intervention in Benjamin Netanyahu's plans to strike Beirut goes to the heart of the relationship between the two sides, in that support may be limited, particularly if it threatens what the US president really wants.

item.title: NSW Police flags move to mandatory use of body-worn cameras after Four Corners investigation
item.description: NSW Police will move to the mandatory use of body-worn cameras for sworn officers in the wake of allegations of a culture of impunity within the police force.

item.title: Second-hand Virginia-class submarines were Australia's first pick for AUKUS
item.description: Australia and the United States' initial positions about delivering a new submarine under AUKUS differed, Senate estimates has revealed.

item.title: Rescue efforts for two missing men in Laos cave shift to vertical tunnels deep in jungle
item.description: Rescue efforts for two remaining men believed to be trapped in a cave in central Laos have shifted to investigating deep vertical tunnels in the jungle.

item.title: Almost one-third of NT children in care allegedly harmed in 2024-25, report finds
item.description: Almost one-third of children in out-of-home care in the Northern Territory were allegedly harmed in the last financial year, according to a new report from the Office of the Children's Commissioner.

item.title: Copper theft on demolition sites now commonplace and costing thousands, contractors say
item.description: Demolition sites are being stripped for copper at an escalating rate, according to a Perth demolition contractor, risking electrocution and asbestos exposure to sell the scrap metal on the black market.

item.title: Under Notre Dame, a 'dig of the century' looks to unearth thousands of years of Parisian history
item.description: More than a year after France's famed Notre Dame reopened, an archaeological exploration going thousands of years into the history of Paris has begun just metres below the cathedral's ground floor.

item.title: Expensive watches, work bags and gold-plated stationery claimed in SA Health scheme
item.description: The Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC) has called for stronger governance of professional development reimbursements for public sector medical officers, with some staff making claims for luxury items like watches and work bags.

item.title: Mitchell Moses's Eels absence raises concerns over potential Origin return
item.description: NSW coach Laurie Daley is facing a crunch call over the make-up of the Blues's spine with Mitchell Moses still sidelined with a hamstring injury.

item.title: Search continues for missing Tasmanian devil at popular Gold Coast wildlife park
item.description: The recently arrived young devil has been kept in a secure indoor facility while quarantining, but was noticed missing Tuesday morning.

item.title: Beer industry shocked by closure of Tasmanian James Boag's brewery
item.description: Beer industry stakeholders say it's a "sad day for the history of Tassie beer" after the announcement Boag's brewery in Launceston will close.

item.title: Inquest considers whether care of Adelaide man's bed sores was 'appropriate and reasonable'
item.description: A coronial inquest will investigate whether an aged care facility in Adelaide's inner north appropriately treated the bed sores a 79-year-old man with dementia developed in the lead-up to his death.

item.title: Prominent obesity surgeon Dr Vahid Reza Adib suspended from practising medicine
item.description: Obesity surgeon Reza Adib has been suspended from practising medicine as he faces rape charges.

item.title: Man charged over weekend double stabbing in Dandenong North and Epping
item.description: The 27-year-old is accused of two separate stabbings on the opposite sides of Melbourne.

item.title: Katter attempts to force discussion on abortion access in Queensland parliament despite ban on debate
item.description: The subject became an issue ahead of the last Queensland election in October 2024, with Premier David Crisafulli implementing a ban on abortion debate after winning government.

item.title: Blasts shake Ukraine in Russian attack
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: One Nation SA says it will not support Labor's bills as it seeks more parliamentary resources
item.description: South Australia's One Nation has vowed to block every Labor government bill following a dispute over staffing allocations.

item.title: Coroner questions parents on use of mobile phone, early morning laundry during infant death inquest
item.description: A coronial inquest is being held into the death of 18-week-old, premature-born twins, who cannot be identified for legal reasons, who died in their Far North Queensland home in late 2021.

item.title: What life is like inside Gaza
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Melbourne Renegades BBL licence to be sold as Cricket Victoria establishes new team
item.description: The Melbourne Renegades BBL licence is set to be sold to a private investor, with Cricket Victoria to establish a new team, marking the end of the Stars as well.

item.title: UN warns countries to prepare for moderate to strong El Niño amid deadly heatwave in India
item.description: India's deadly heatwave comes as the UN warns the world to prepare for a moderate to strong El Niño this year.

item.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon amid ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: Lebanese state media has reported Israeli strikes, some deadly, at about 30 locations in the country's south despite a partial ceasefire.

item.title: Ralph Jackman's memoir about his time as a teacher in a youth detention centre reveals a broken system
item.description: After Ralph Jackman retrained as a teacher, his first job was in a youth detention centre. His new memoir reveals how a broken system is failing vulnerable kids.

item.title: Gender euphoria behind the mic: Kae Tempest refuses to let transphobia slow him down
item.description: Poet, rapper, musician, playwright and novelist Kae Tempest is a prodigious polymath. It wasn't until he affirmed his gender that he connected with his true self.

item.title: Travel quiz, June 3: Hold on to your hat, we're off on a tour of the globe
item.description: The famous US road trip along Route 66 links which two cities? And which country eats the most garlic per person?

item.title: Almost eight months on from the ceasefire, Gaza's residents are still struggling to rebuild a city in ruins
item.description: It's been seven months since the ceasefire in Gaza, but reconstruction has barely begun, water remains contaminated and minimal aid is getting into the strip. This is what life is like for its residents.

item.title: Australian-made rocket motor designed for missiles test-fired for first time
item.description: In what is being described as a significant leap forward in Australia's push to develop and manufacture its own missiles, newly released footage shows the first test-firing of a locally made solid rocket motor.

item.title: Three Australian men found guilty after taking 'black flight' into Indonesia's South Papua province
item.description: Three Australian men have been found guilty by a District Court in Merauke after taking a "black flight" into Indonesia's South Papua province from Far North Queensland without visas.

item.title: Australians have become housing hostages. Will the budget set them free?
item.description: Before the federal budget, the housing market was already starting to choke on its own excesses. Now some economists are tipping an extended period where home prices grow no faster than general inflation.

item.title: We asked Google AI about big tobacco. Its answer was missing some controversial history
item.description: We searched Google for information on some of the world's biggest tobacco companies. The results raised questions about how much of the AI overview was coming from the brands themselves.

item.title: Snowy 2.0 tunnel breakthrough
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Billions over budget and years late: How Snowy 2.0 got here
item.description: Australia's most ambitious renewable energy project is slowly nearing completion, but experts are divided on whether Snowy 2.0 will ever justify its snowballing price tag.

item.title: Heavy-lift vessel Black Marlin headed to Hobart to pick up Incat electric ferry for South America
item.description: The 217-metre-long heavy-lift vessel Black Marlin will deliver a Tasmanian shipbuilder's new electric ferry to South America, after the previous transporter became caught up in the Strait of Hormuz closure.

item.title: Tasmanians letting fitness regimes slip as cost-of-living pressures rise
item.description: As household budgets tighten, keeping up a health and fitness regime often slips, but experts say giving people cheap or free ways to stay fit is critical to longer-term wellbeing.

item.title: Quilpie builders experiment with 'flat-pack' housing to improve supply in the outback
item.description: A Queensland outback town pins its hopes of solving a local housing crisis on "flat-pack" homes as the high cost of building risks paralysing the community.

item.title: SA Voice damaged by advertising failures and voter confusion, FOI documents show
item.description: New documents suggest South Australia's inaugural Voice to Parliament election was hamstrung by advertising failures that left First Nations voters confused or unaware an election was taking place.

item.title: Mango twig tip dieback spreading in the NT as more trees get bulldozed
item.description: At a field day in the Northern Territory, mango growers were desperate to learn what could be done to stop the spread of mango twig tip dieback.

item.title: Reports of wildlife displaced at Fred Caterson Reserve as controversial sports project begins
item.description: A sports development in Sydney's north-west has been accused by residents of killing and displacing wildlife.

item.title: Marta Kostyuk praises Daria Kasatkina ahead of French Open semifinal against Russia's Mirra Andreeva
item.description: Ukrainian star Marta Kostyuk says it is unacceptable for Russians to stay silent on the war, especially after Daria Kasatkina's defection to Australia.

item.title: Australian Opal Centre receives $1 million government lifeline as it scales back to survive
item.description: Construction of the museum boasting the world's greatest public collection of opalised dinosaur fossils has been on hold since the project ran out of money last year.

item.title: Bodycam vision shows UK police handcuffing stabbed man
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Starmer condemns Henry Nowak's death as police response sparks Southampton race protests
item.description: The British prime minister said he "felt sick" while watching video released by police of 18-year-old Henry Nowak, who was handcuffed while he lay dying from stab wounds after his killer falsely alleged a racist attack.

item.title: Red skies, rainbows and a ravaging storm — see WA's best weather photos for May
item.description: May delivered Western Australia bursts of sunshine, brief spells of rain and long stretches of dry skies that left many wondering when winter would finally arrive — until it did with a bang.

item.title: Australia levels ODI series in Pakistan as Nathan Ellis stars with the ball
item.description: Nathan Ellis shines with the ball, while Cameron Green tops the run-scoring for Australia in a 41-run ODI victory over Pakistan in Lahore.

item.title: Young cancer patient's stem cell transplant struggle highlights shortage of local donors
item.description: Sarah Taylor is one of approximately 1,200 Australians urgently waiting for a life-saving stem cell transplant. Advocates say Australia's registry is "ridiculously small" compared to other nations, leading to potentially fatal consequences.

item.title: Donald Trump appoints ally Bill Pulte as acting US intelligence director
item.description: Bill Pulte, who is known for publicly attacking the US president's political enemies, would also retain his existing jobs overseeing federal housing and mortgage policies.

item.title: Former Canberra Liberals leader Leanne Castley leaves party, citing 'toxic culture, threat of physical assault'
item.description: Former Canberra Liberals leader Leanne Castley says she has experienced "bullying, intimidation, lies and a threat of physical assault" in the past 18 months.

item.title: 'A lot more headwinds to come' as economy slows, consumers turn 'cautious'
item.description: Australia's economy expanded 0.3 per cent in the first quarter of 2026, taking the annual growth rate to 2.5 per cent, the ABS says. The ASX rose. Look back on how the session unfolded.

item.title: Medicare bulk-billing model forces Kalbarri Doctors Surgery closure, director says
item.description: Kalbarri residents will be forced to travel more than 100 kilometres to visit a doctor when the regional WA town's only GP clinic closes due to financial pressure.

item.title: Protesters clash with police in Southampton over murder of Henry Nowak
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Queensland crackdown cuts illicit tobacco and vape shops, but black market adapts
item.description: Queensland's crackdown has reduced visible illicit tobacco sales, according to a public health researcher, but the black market has adapted, prompting calls for the tobacco excise to be cut.

item.title: Barnaby Joyce speaks at Sydney rally ahead of bill to outlaw sex-selective abortions
item.description: Nine months out from the NSW election campaign, One Nation recruit Barnaby Joyce has appeared in Sydney, declaring politicians are fearful of losing their jobs.

item.title: Sheepskin market dramatically rebounds amid tighter supply and Chinese demand
item.description: Just 12 months ago, Australian sheepskins were worthless and being dumped in landfill. They are now in hot demand amid a surging merino wool industry.

item.title: Amazon, Temu and AliExpress sell banned kids products in Australia
item.description: Products that may already be banned in Australia are being sold by retail giants including Amazon, Temu, AliExpress and eBay.

item.title: New political candidates to benefit under changes to Victoria's electoral act
item.description: Six months out from the state election, Victoria had no rules governing donations due to the High Court finding in April that the laws were unconstitutional.

item.title: Police remove car after it crashes into Hyde Park's Archibald Fountain
item.description: The CBD fountain sustained minimal damage after a French national allegedly drove into it early this morning.

item.title: Search continues for missing Tasmanian devil 'Mary'
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: US military footage appears to show strike on Kharg island-bound vessel
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Iranian missiles strike Kuwait International Airport as US hits Qeshm Island
item.description: At least one person has been killed and more than 60 injured in an attack on Kuwait's international airport, as Iran and the US launch strikes against each other.

item.title: UK Athletics fined for 'wholly avoidable' death of Paralympian Abdullah Hayayei
item.description: Abdullah Hayayei, from the United Arab Emirates, was preparing for the 2017 World Paralympic Athletics Championships at the Newham Leisure Centre when a metal throwing cage fell on him.

item.title: Defamation case involving former Carlton president Luke Sayers to be heard publicly after failed court bid
item.description: A bid by Mr Sayers to have a defamation case brought by his wife over a nude photo scandal heard privately has been rejected by a judge.

item.title: Angry swarm of bees surrounds hive truck rollover on Warrego Highway west of Roma
item.description: Angry bees are buzzing around a truck carrying hives after it crashed on the Warrego Highway this morning.

item.title: Australia's economy growing at 2.5 per cent annually as slowdown begins
item.description: Australia's economy grew at an annual rate of 2.5 per cent in the March quarter, the same as in the previous quarter.

item.title: Solomon Islands agrees to treaty negotiation with Australia
item.description: The new Solomon Islands PM has made a clear pivot towards Australia, agreeing to kickstart negotiations on a comprehensive treaty and promising to "review" the Pacific nation's contentious security agreement with China.

item.title: Victoria's Game Management Authority accused of ignoring duck hunting breaches, bullying opponents
item.description: The Game Management Authority is accused of turning a blind eye to illegal behaviour by shooters while bullying wildlife rescuers and anti-hunting campaigners.

item.title: Fremantle Dockers AFL player Nathan O'Driscoll says mental health admission 'best thing' he did
item.description: Fremantle Dockers midfielder Nathan O'Driscoll says he has received "nothing but love" after posting on social media about his struggles with anxiety and how he contemplated taking his own life.

item.title: 1 million tonnes of fertiliser imported into Australia since Iran war began
item.description: Streamlined biosecurity checks of fertiliser shipments have fast-tracked imports of urea for Australia's winter crop.

item.title: Peabo Bryson, A Whole New World and Beauty and the Beast singer, dies
item.description: Peabo Bryson, known for his Grammy and Oscar-winning Disney collaborations, has died at 75.

item.title: Five ways to prevent mould in your home and the best ways to clean it
item.description: Winter is here and with it comes the increased risk of mould in homes, particularly in colder parts of the country. But there are simple ways to keep to it at bay.

item.title: The Unpackit Awards name the 'worst offenders' in Australian packaging
item.description: A trendy plastic-and-metal takeaway cup used by cafes for iced drinks has been named Australia's worst packaging in the first Unpackit Awards.

item.title: Border Force busts multi-million-dollar illicit tobacco operation in the Northern Territory
item.description: Australian Border Force officers have seized hundreds of thousands of illegal cigarettes and vapes worth millions of dollars from a Northern Territory storage facility.

item.title: Tasmania Police charge 43-year-old man over 3D-printed firearm operation
item.description: An operation allegedly producing illegal 3D-printed guns has been uncovered and shut down in southern Tasmania.

item.title: Authorities 'deeply sorry' for teenager let down by WA's mental health system
item.description: The WA health minister and director of the state's country health service have unreservedly apologised to a 17-year-old left in an emergency room for 116 hours waiting for specialised mental health care.

item.title: Reece Walsh opens up on Maroons omission for State of Origin I against Blues
item.description: Reece Walsh says he understands why he was left out of Queensland's State of Origin squad for the series opener.

item.title: Pair found guilty of killing Greenbushes man Raymond Smith during home burglary
item.description: Two men are convicted of killing 68-year-old Raymond Smith in his own home in Western Australia's South West during a meth-addled burglary for drugs.

item.title: CCTV captures people entering New York sewers at night
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police investigating people entering New York sewers for unknown reason
item.description: Security cameras have recorded at least three night-time instances where groups of people entered or exited sewer tunnels via maintenance holes on streets in Brooklyn and Queens.

item.title: Underworld figure Mick Gatto released without charge following arrest
item.description: Mick Gatto has been released without charge after being arrested by Victorian police investigating "alleged financial offences" earlier today.

item.title: Duo arrested following alleged crime spree around Mildura and cross-border pursuit
item.description: Victorian police say two people have been arrested over an alleged crime spree and cross-border pursuit in the Sunraysia region.

item.title: Paedophile Peter Liddy's release from jail prompts vigilante warning from police
item.description: South Australia's police chief says the force will respond accordingly to concerns about public safety once paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy is released from prison, but added there would be no tolerance for "vigilante activity".

item.title: What the looming sale of Melbourne Renegades means for fans and the BBL
item.description: If the Renegades are sold to a private buyer as expected, what could it mean for Melbourne fans if the BBL team no longer looks like itself?

item.title: Plan to relocate Robertson's Big Potato rejected by Wingecarribee Council
item.description: One of Australia's most infamous "big things" could be moved to make way for a new supermarket in the NSW Southern Highlands.

item.title: Influential Aboriginal leader Lowitja O'Donoghue immortalised in bronze statue
item.description: A bronze statue of influential Aboriginal leader Lowitja O'Donoghue has been unveiled in Adelaide — the first of six monuments of prominent Indigenous South Australians that the state government has planned.

item.title: Anthony Albanese once fought to protect Sydney Harbour land government plans to sell
item.description: Parliamentary speeches from 2021 reveal the prime minister was opposed to privatising Sydney Harbour land now being sold off by the Defence Department.

item.title: No charges for Mark Bird, who Tasmanian coroner found faked wife Helen's suicide
item.description: A man who a coroner found made his wife's 2010 death look like a suicide will not face prosecution after police said there was "insufficient evidence" to lay charges.

item.title: Queensland Reds coach Les Kiss confident ahead of Super Rugby Pacific finals
item.description: Queensland Reds coach Les Kiss knows his side will be underdogs in this weekend's Super Rugby qualifying final, a match that could be his last with the club.

item.title: Members of NT Legislative Scrutiny Committee call for shake-up after recommendations rejected
item.description: Non-government members of the Northern Territory Legislative Scrutiny Committee are calling for the body to be reformed, after the NT government ignored all 11 recommendations it made to improve a recent bill.

item.title: Teen left with fractured skull after e-scooter crash, raising 'genuine concern' over e-devices
item.description: Two separate e-device accidents, one of which left a 15-year-old girl with a fractured skull, has raised "genuine" concern about the use of such devices a month out of a planned SA government review.

item.title: Fears of job losses as NBN confirms overhaul of television bulletins
item.description: Media academics fear for the future of regional journalism after the drastic downsizing of one of Australia's oldest nightly news bulletins.

item.title: Man fined for booing during Anzac Day dawn service at Sydney's Martin Place
item.description: Eli Toby told the court that his actions were intended as a way to "stand for what I believed was the right thing at the time", but refused to apologise to the judge.

item.title: Neo-Nazi leader Thomas Sewell covertly broadcasting hate on Instagram from Melbourne acreage
item.description: ABC NEWS Verify can reveal that Neo-Nazi leader Thomas Sewell has been uploading antisemitic and homophobic diatribes to Instagram from a property on the outskirts of Melbourne.

item.title: Australian diver Josh Richards leaves Laos cave rescue
item.description: Josh Richards is on his way back to Australia, after multiple days helping rescue miners trapped in a Laos cave.

item.title: Boag's brewery worker 'angry' and 'frustrated' as Launceston braces for $50 million economic blow
item.description: The loss of James Boag's brewery and Hawthorn Football Club to northern Tasmania's economy could cost the region a million dollars a week, local leaders say.

item.title: Sydney's 2SER community radio to avoid closure under reduced model
item.description: The 46-year-old Sydney community radio institution was facing closure after around half its core funding was withdrawn late last year by Macquarie University.

item.title: People with intellectual disability continue to receive substandard healthcare as program to train doctors risks closure
item.description: Sam Stubbs’s parents were asked about his quality of life while treating him for a lung infection. They realised it was because he was born with Down syndrome.

item.title: KPMG Australia chief operating officer Eileen Hoggett steps aside as audit leak fallout widens
item.description: KPMG Australia chief operating officer Eileen Hoggett will step aside from her role while investigations are pending into the audit leak scandal at the big four firm.

item.title: Missing Tasmanian devil will likely survive as Gold Coast search goes into second day
item.description: Sniffer dogs and thermal imaging drones are being deployed as the search for a missing Tasmanian devil on the Gold Coast goes into a second day.

item.title: Brisbane man charged under new state laws after allegedly plotting violent attack on family member
item.description: A court heard Atallah Mohaghegh Nahavandi, 61, sought out a third party to assault his ex-wife's nephew "so he cannot talk anymore".

item.title: Water Corp defends response to leaks and sewage spills amid flood of complaints
item.description: For two years, residents in one of the driest parts of Western Australia have watched thousands of litres of water leaking from a Water Corp-owned tank.

item.title: One person in custody after stabbing at Grand Central Shopping Centre in Toowoomba
item.description: A man in his 20s is being treated for arm injuries after a stabbing in a busy regional Queensland shopping centre.

item.title: Hollywood legend set to bring outback Finke Desert Race to the big screen
item.description: The gruelling outback Finke Desert Race is set for Hollywood treatment after Marvel's Chris Hemsworth backs a feature-length movie.

item.title: Dramatic Darwin arrest of 33yo man wanted for breach of parole over Hungry Jack's incident
item.description: Northern Territory police have carried out a dramatic arrest of a 33-year-old man on a main Darwin road, saying the fugitive was wanted for a breach of parole.

item.title: Ali Brigginshaw steps down as Broncos NRLW skipper, opens up on Gorden Tallis drama
item.description: Ali Brigginshaw relinquishes the Broncos captaincy in her final NRLW season and reveals her view about a club meeting room being renamed in her honour.

item.title: Gold reserves at Kalgoorlie Super Pit continue to grow with millions spent on underground drilling
item.description: A big exploration push by Australia's biggest gold miner, Northern Star Resources, has grown the reserves at the Super Pit by 54 per cent in the past six years.

item.title: ABS estimates 80pc of tobacco consumed in Australia last year illegal amid 'rapid growth' in black market
item.description: The experimental estimates are based on "nicotine metabolite concentrations" detected in wastewater samples, combined with data showing a collapse in spending on legal cigarettes and vapes.

item.title: Can one optimal pathway have two lanes? When it comes to AUKUS submarines, apparently yes
item.description: Scrutiny of AUKUS's delivery reveals two "optimal pathways" for Australia's submarines.

item.title: The bushland where a Tasmanian Devil is believed to be missing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sydney car crash aftermath
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police allege corporate boss refused blood alcohol tests after Sydney car crash
item.description: Private equity baron Joel Thickins is alleged to have crashed his BMW into five cars in Sydney's east, before refusing two alcohol-detecting breath tests.

item.title: Sydney Morning Herald takes down opinion piece about AI after author used AI to write it
item.description: The Sydney Morning Herald has taken down an opinion piece defending the use of artificial intelligence at universities, after it emerged its author used AI to write it.

item.title: Electric vehicle sales in Australia hit new high in May
item.description: The monthly sales record comes as nearly half of all new vehicles sold last month were either an EV, hybrid, or plug-in hybrid.

item.title: Tyrendarra Football Netball Club bans sex offender after female footballers abandon club
item.description: A south-west Victorian football club has apologised after it allowed a convicted sex offender to return to the club after a stint in jail.

item.title: Latest search for missing SA mother Trisha Graf fails to locate fresh evidence
item.description: After three days of searching, South Australian police are no closer to finding out what happened to missing mother of two Trisha Graf.

item.title: John Setka faces court over alleged threatening emails to CFMEU administrators
item.description: Former construction union boss John Setka has faced court fighting allegations he sent abusive emails, with his lawyer saying he has received threats on his life.

item.title: Mother granted bail after allegedly trying to print child abuse material at Canberra store
item.description: The woman's defence argues her daughter took and tried to print the photographs herself for a creative project and they were not predatory in nature.

item.title: Alice Springs's Tangentyere CEO Walter Shaw defends state of town camp housing
item.description: After the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, the state of Alice Springs's town camps shocked the nation. Now, the man in charge of servicing them is speaking out.

item.title: Man dies in custody after crashing ute into Mudgee police station
item.description: A man has died in a maximum security prison days after driving his car at police officers, narrowly missing them, before crashing into a police station in Western New South Wales.

item.title: Wednesday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Australian Federal Police intercept luxury yacht, allege three people try to help Queensland fugitive
item.description: WA Police and Australian Federal Police intercepted a yacht off the coast of Exmouth in northern WA which allegedly had a Queensland fugitive on board.

item.title: Childcare worker Edwina Ling sentenced to four years' jail for assaulting toddlers
item.description: Queensland childcare worker Edwina Ling has been jailed for four years after pleading guilty to more than 80 counts of assault involving three young children.

item.title: Ukraine drones hit St Petersburg, while NATO chief makes unannounced Kyiv visit
item.description: An oil export terminal was hit hours before the Russian president's annual economic forum got underway.

item.title: Australia faces proposed 12.5pc US tariff over forced labour crackdown
item.description: Australia could be hit with a new US tariff after the Trump administration accused dozens of countries of failing to crack down on goods made with forced labour.

item.title: Former head of Indonesian free school meals program arrested by the attorney general
item.description: The ex-chief of Indonesia's free school meals program linked to food poisoning cases has been arrested after a corruption probe.
